_comment: GENERATED from CATALOGS.md by tools/gen-contract.py — do not edit by hand; edit the prose and regenerate.

failure_shapes.name: Anchor-and-drift — surrounding context composed while the anchor is fetched
failure_shapes.fence: per-source attestation + three-state citation + verification stack

failure_shapes.name: Within-source attribution swap (figures correct, mapping reversed)
Cite-through fabrication shielded by source authority — the cite-through is structurally correct but the cited-from passage is subtly strengthened beyond what the source states
failure_shapes.fence: per-source attestation with quote-context anchors + adversarial-read quote-context walk (sharpens GR.4's fence for the strengthening-via-authority pattern)

failure_shapes.name: Source unfetched — a training-recalled claim presented as cited
Composed claims with no source attempt (effort estimates, counts, comparatives)
Quote-context distortion — quote accurate, surrounding qualifier stripped
Thin attestation passing lint — frontmatter correct, body un-anchored
Attestation-abuse — attestation form is correct (anchors present, key passages transcribed) but authored without genuine source engagement; lint and thin-attestation checks both pass
failure_shapes.fence: bracket directive (partial) + adversarial-read gloss-fit job (attestation-paraphrase vs independent source-summary) — detection pending

failure_shapes.name: Paradigm-trained gestalt failure — per-claim substrate is accurate, but the agent's gestalt of the whole source fits a trained category rather than the source's own argument structure
failure_shapes.fence: bracket directive (partial) + adversarial-read independent-gestalt job (read the source independently, compare gestalts) — detection pending

failure_shapes.name: Noise domination — less-relevant retrieved content used over more-relevant
Coverage gaps / unknown-unknowns
Stale substrate — once-correct content now out of date
Smoothed contradictions in cross-source synthesis

Premature normalization of abnormal discourse — a substrate-surfaced novel framing is translated into existing vocabulary before its abnormality is adequately surfaced; the normalized form passes the whole stack

Cross-tradition convergence-smoothing — multiple traditions' framings of a convergent shape merged into one vocabulary, losing per-tradition prescriptive content (harder to detect than CO.1: the surface signal is agreement, not disagreement)
failure_shapes.fence: encoder-side translation-table discipline (each tradition's term preserved; convergence named at the shape-diagnosed level; framings stand side-by-side) + tradition-isolation adversarial-read job — detection pending

failure_shapes.name: Self-confirming framing
Premature commitment — a position asserted before its grounding substrate is engaged
Drift-inheritance through paraphrase chains — small per-step distortion compounding across multi-step composition
Decomposition-framing lock-in — a facet cut inherits a lens all downstream work runs inside
failure_shapes.fence: multi-sampling at decompose (≥3 candidates + comparative + bracket) + persisted rationale

failure_shapes.name: Per-artifact-warrant fallacy — an artifact passes per-artifact checks but the corpus context that grounded its claims has shifted (warrant is corpus-distributed, not per-artifact)
Theory-ladenness of substrate-test outputs — the substrate test is itself paradigm-conditioned; framings the framework's paradigm naturalizes stay invisible to in-paradigm verification

Consumer-side decoding-frame mismatch — an artifact's encoded intent (held-pending vs settled; substrate vs deliverable) is mis-received by a downstream consumer; the encoder's stack catches encoding-side failures, not consumer-side decoding

source_classes.load_bearing: version + section anchors (revise at section granularity)

pattern_categories.name: Decimals with source attributions
pattern_categories.description: a decimal figure adjacent to a source name; highest empirical failure rate. Flags proximity for verification.

pattern_categories.name: Version numbers
pattern_categories.description: version identifiers in comparative/attributional claims without an in-corpus anchor.

pattern_categories.name: File / word / page counts
pattern_categories.description: numeric counts with units and no citation.

pattern_categories.name: Comparative superlatives
pattern_categories.description: "the only X with Y", "the strongest baseline" — claims requiring a full-set survey the agent did not perform.

pattern_categories.name: Named-feature claims
pattern_categories.description: capabilities attributed to a tool/system by name without a fetched anchor.

pattern_categories.name: Composed effort estimates
pattern_categories.description: quantitative effort claims constructed without substrate (forbidden per SPEC.md §4.4).

decision_points.name: evaluate
decision_points.conditional: selectable; reachable only when a cross-synthesis artifact exists

decision_points.name: user-validation
decision_points.conditional: when the user is the committing party

decision_points.name: promote
decision_points.conditional: when converged substrate warrants cross-arc lifting

provenance_values.applies_to: reference raws, indices, per-piece notes, vocab captures
cross-source analytical artifacts (briefs, syntheses, positions, ledgers)

typed_edge_predicates.semantic: Generic citation (root of the citation-predicate tree)
Cites as a source of factual evidence
Builds on prior work with new content
Explicit disagreement; the cited claim is shown false
Adopts a methodology from the cited work
Evidence backs a claim (research grounds a decision)
Generic associative link when no stronger type fits (the only symmetric predicate)
An implementation realizes a design / decision
